deep_dive.title: Conditional Optimal Bridge for Riemannian Activation Steering
deep_dive.abstract: Activation steering offers a lightweight alternative to fine-tuning for controlling large language models at inference time. While many existing methods implicitly optimize a log-density-ratio objective between desired and undesired activation distributions, they do so heuristically rather than deriving it from a principled optimization problem. Moreover, these methods produce query-independent steering directions that can degrade performance on both in-distribution and out-of-distribution (OOD) inputs. We introduce \textsc{Cobras} (Conditional Optimal Bridge for Riemannian Activation Steering), which addresses both limitations by casting activation steering as a Schrödinger Bridge on the residual-stream hypersphere. This formulation yields, to our knowledge, the first principled derivation of the log-density-ratio steering objective from a well-posed optimization problem. Solving the bridge via entropic optimal transport and extracting the probability flow ODE recovers the widely used density-ratio gradient as a special case when the Sinkhorn potentials are uniform. Crucially, the Schrödinger potentials are evaluated at the current activation, making the resulting steering direction inherently query-adaptive. Empirically, across four models and three alignment axes (helpfulness, truthfulness, and detoxification), \textsc{Cobras} consistently outperforms prior activation steering baselines while avoiding the OOD degradation commonly observed in existing methods. The code can be found at https://github.com/arshandalili/cobras.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: formulates activation steering as a Schrodinger bridge on the residual-stream hypersphere
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 将激活转向建模为残差流超球面上的薛定谔桥问题
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 将激活转向建模为残差流超球面上的薛定谔桥问题
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 将激活转向建模为残差流超球面上的薛定谔桥问题
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents Cobras, which casts activation steering in LLMs as a Schrodinger bridge on a hypersphere, deriving query-dependent steering directions from an optimal transport formulation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型激活转向方法缺乏理论依据且方向固定导致分布外性能下降的问题，该工作将激活转向形式化为残差流超球面上的薛定谔桥问题。通过求解该最优传输问题，得到依赖于查询的转向方向。理论推导和实验表明，该方法优于现有启发式方法，在分布内和分布外输入上均保持性能。首次将薛定谔桥应用于语言模型控制，展示了随机桥过程在条件生成中的潜力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对大语言模型激活转向方法缺乏理论依据且方向固定导致分布外性能下降的问题，该工作将激活转向形式化为残差流超球面上的薛定谔桥问题。通过求解该最优传输问题，得到依赖于查询的转向方向。理论推导和实验表明，该方法优于现有启发式方法，在分布内和分布外输入上均保持性能。首次将薛定谔桥应用于语言模型控制，展示了随机桥过程在条件生成中的潜力。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Schrödinger bridge problem in stochastic processes

deep_dive.title: Markov Chain Monte Carlo with Diffusion Paths
deep_dive.abstract: Sampling from multimodal distributions is a longstanding challenge for classical local Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) methods. A popular remedy is to introduce a sequence of intermediate distributions that interpolate between the target and a simpler reference. The classical choice, tempering, raises the density to a power, but distorts the relative weights of asymmetric modes and can lead to poor mixing. We instead propose interpolating along the diffusion path, the marginals of a noising diffusion process that carries the target toward a Gaussian. This path preserves the relative weights of the modes and enjoys favorable mixing properties, which we make precise through a spectral-gap analysis of the corresponding ideal transition kernel. Sampling along the path requires its intermediate scores, which can be estimated from the unnormalized target through variational approaches, yielding only an approximate sampler. To remove the resulting bias, we introduce the Metropolis-adjusted diffusion path (MAD-Path) sampler, which corrects the diffusion-path proposal in an augmented path space and leaves the target invariant regardless of the accuracy of the learned score or the discretization error. We further quantify how these two errors affect the acceptance probability, providing guidance for practical tuning. Experiments on a range of Bayesian posteriors show that MAD-Path improves global exploration and mode-weight estimation relative to tempering-based MCMC methods and unadjusted diffusion samplers.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: diffusion path interpolation between target and reference distributions
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 扩散路径在目标分布和参考分布之间插值
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 扩散路径在目标分布和参考分布之间插值
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 扩散路径在目标分布和参考分布之间插值
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes using diffusion paths as intermediate distributions for MCMC, preserving mode weights and enabling efficient sampling, conceptually equivalent to constructing a diffusion bridge.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出利用扩散路径（即噪声扩散过程的边缘分布）在目标分布和高斯参考分布之间插值，用于马尔可夫链蒙特卡洛采样。该方法保留了多峰分布中模式的相对权重，具有更好的混合性质，并通过谱间隙分析证明了理想转移核的收敛速度。这本质上是一种扩散桥的构造，能够连接任意两个分布，为生成建模和采样提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出利用扩散路径（即噪声扩散过程的边缘分布）在目标分布和高斯参考分布之间插值，用于马尔可夫链蒙特卡洛采样。该方法保留了多峰分布中模式的相对权重，具有更好的混合性质，并通过谱间隙分析证明了理想转移核的收敛速度。这本质上是一种扩散桥的构造，能够连接任意两个分布，为生成建模和采样提供了新思路。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: diffusion bridge connecting two probability distributions

quick_skim.title: Sticky Jump Diffusions: A Unifying View of Masked, Continuous, and Hybrid Diffusion
quick_skim.abstract: We introduce Sticky Jump Diffusions (SJDs), continuous-time Markov processes on $\mathbb R^d$ whose discrete anchors are token embeddings. In forward time, anchors release their mass at a hazard rate and the released mass diffuses in the continuous ambient space; time reversal couples a score-driven SDE with a sticky jump kernel whose rate and destination are fixed by flux balance with the forward law. We estimate the score and the per-anchor reverse hazards from a single denoising classifier via Denoising Hazard Matching, the hazard analogue of denoising score matching, with simulation-free cross-entropy training. SJD recovers masked diffusion, continuous diffusion, and hybrid diffusion as limits. Its reversal explains features that each family treats as given: the mask of masked diffusion carries no evidence about the source token because the unsticking kernel of every anchor collapses to the same absorbing point; the terminal projection of continuous diffusion is required due to the absence of atoms in its forward marginal, without which flux balance yields no reverse jumps; and the update rules of hybrid diffusion (commit rate, destination, and drift) all follow from flux balance rather than from separate design. Beyond these limits, the unsticking kernel becomes a design space: a cross-position blending corrupts each position toward a blend of its neighbors' clean values or embeddings, turning dependency structure such as spatial locality or a constraint graph into an inductive bias of the corruption itself, and improves over the identity-kernel hybrid on CIFAR-10, Text8, and Sudoku.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Sticky Jump Diffusions unify diffusion paradigms with time reversal and score-driven SDE
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 粘性跳跃扩散统一了扩散范式，包含时间反转和分数驱动SDE，与随机桥过程类似
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 粘性跳跃扩散统一了扩散范式，包含时间反转和分数驱动SDE，与随机桥过程类似
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 粘性跳跃扩散统一了扩散范式，包含时间反转和分数驱动SDE，与随机桥过程类似
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Sticky Jump Diffusions (SJDs) are continuous-time Markov processes that unify masked, continuous, and hybrid diffusion via a score-driven SDE with a sticky jump kernel; time reversal couples flux balance, enabling a unifying generative framework relevant to stochastic bridge processes.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出粘性跳跃扩散（SJD），一种连续时间马尔可夫过程，统一了掩码扩散、连续扩散和混合扩散。正向过程中离散锚点以风险率释放质量，质量在连续空间中扩散；时间反转通过通量平衡耦合分数驱动SDE和粘性跳跃核。该方法通过去噪危险匹配进行训练，无需模拟。SJD变体可恢复现有扩散模型，其反向过程为构建随机桥过程提供了统一视角，对生成建模具有潜在价值。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出粘性跳跃扩散（SJD），一种连续时间马尔可夫过程，统一了掩码扩散、连续扩散和混合扩散。正向过程中离散锚点以风险率释放质量，质量在连续空间中扩散；时间反转通过通量平衡耦合分数驱动SDE和粘性跳跃核。该方法通过去噪危险匹配进行训练，无需模拟。SJD变体可恢复现有扩散模型，其反向过程为构建随机桥过程提供了统一视角，对生成建模具有潜在价值。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: stochastic bridge processes for generative modeling
